At 11:29 the sense is specifically MATERIAL RELIEF (famine-money-food) which requires 'การช่วยเหลือ' (help/aid) — matching the 2 COR 8:4, 9:1, 9:12-13 pattern where διακονία in collection-contexts = physical-aid-ministry. Glossary-contextual-variant logged for διακονία-in-relief-contexts. Thai 'การช่วยเหลือ' clearly communicates relief-aid-sense.

key_decisions.greek: τοῖς κατοικοῦσιν ἐν τῇ Ἰουδαίᾳ ἀδελφοῖς
key_decisions.thai: พี่น้องที่อาศัยอยู่ในแคว้นยูเดีย
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-metaphor (note at v.29): 'brothers' = fellow-believers. Thai 'พี่น้อง' locked. κατοικέω → อาศัยอยู่ (dwell-reside, standard). The Judea-resident-brothers are the Jerusalem-area believers, directly-affected by the famine. Note symbolic-importance: Gentile-majority Antioch-church sending aid to Jewish-majority Judean-church demonstrates the single-unified-church that the earlier chapters have been establishing theologically.

translation.notes: 1 HAPAX: εὐπορέω (v.29 prosper/have-resources → มีกำลัง, NT-only; classical 'fare-well/have-resources'). Per uW figs-explicit + figs-metaphor: proportional-ability + brothers-as-fellow-believers. διακονία contextual-variant at 11:29 → การช่วยเหลือ (vs. Luke-Acts-locked 'พันธกิจ'), documented as a ministry-of-material-aid sense distinct from apostolic-ministry sense. Theologically-significant first-ever Gentile-to-Jewish church-to-church financial-solidarity. No textual variant.

greek: ὃ καὶ ἐποίησαν ἀποστείλαντες πρὸς τοὺς πρεσβυτέρους διὰ χειρὸς Βαρναβᾶ καὶ Σαύλου.
bsb_english: This they did, sending their gifts to the elders with Barnabas and Saul.

translation.thai: พวกเขาก็ได้ทำเช่นนั้น โดยส่งการถวายไปให้บรรดาผู้ปกครองคริสตจักรผ่านทางบารนาบัสและเซาโล

key_decisions.thai: ให้บรรดาผู้ปกครองคริสตจักร
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-explicit (note at v.30): the 'elders' = leaders-of-the-church-in-Jerusalem. Thai 'บรรดาผู้ปกครองคริสตจักร' (the-church-elders/overseers) makes the ecclesiastical-referent explicit. πρεσβύτερος → ผู้ปกครองคริสตจักร (standard Thai church-elder rendering, matches ACT 14:23, 15:2, 15:4, 15:6, 15:22-23, 16:4, 20:17, 21:18, 1 TIM 5:17, 5:19, TIT 1:5, JAM 5:14, 1 PET 5:1, 5:5). This is the FIRST appearance of πρεσβύτεροι as a church-office-designation (distinct from the standard 'elders-of-Israel' sense used for Jewish-Sanhedrin-elders earlier). The ecclesiastical-sense implies structured-leadership-succession already established in Jerusalem ~10-15 years after the cross. Note: Luke does not name the ἀποστόλοι as recipients — by ~AD 45-46 the apostolic-leadership has diversified into apostles + elders (cf. ACT 15:2 'ἀποστόλους καὶ πρεσβυτέρους').

key_decisions.thai: ผ่านทางบารนาβัςและเซาโล
key_decisions.rationale: Per uW figs-synecdoche + grammar-collectivenouns (notes at v.30): 'hand' synecdoche for personal-agency; collective-hand could be plural. Thai 'ผ่านทาง' (by-way-of/through) natural-synecdoche-carrier for agency-through-person (avoids the awkward-Thai 'มือของ' which would sound anatomical). The Thai idiom preserves Greek's agency-by-X structure without forcing the literal-body-part. Βαρναβᾶ καὶ Σαύλου — note the Barnabas-first ordering throughout 11:25-30, 12:25, 13:1-2, 13:7 until the Cyprus-Paphos conversion-of-Sergius-Paulus at 13:9 after which Luke-shifts to 'Παῦλος καὶ Βαρναβᾶς' (Paul-first) 13:13, 13:43, 13:46, 13:50, 14:20, 15:2, 15:22, 15:35. Thai preserves Greek-ordering here at 11:30 'บารนาβัςและเซาโล' (Barnabas-first) — matches mentoring-relationship phase.

key_decisions.thai: ส่งการถวาย
key_decisions.rationale: ἀποστέλλω → ส่ง (standard-send). 'การถวาย' (the-offering/gift) renders the implied-gift-object of ἀποστείλαντες (the relief-funds from v.29) — Thai 'การถวาย' (offering-in-giving-upward-register) preserves the reverent-gift-to-church-elders-register rather than bare 'ของ' (thing/item) or 'เงิน' (money). Matches LUK 21:4 widow's-mite 'τὰ δῶρα → การถวาย' + ACT 2:45, 4:34-37 community-of-goods giving-register.

translation.notes: Per uW figs-explicit + figs-synecdoche + grammar-collectivenouns: elders-as-Jerusalem-leaders + hand-as-agency + collective-hand-possibly-plural. The Barnabas-Saul joint-commission foreshadows the Pauline-missionary-journeys (ACT 13:1ff.). The shift from apostolic-sole-leadership (ACT 1-6) to apostles + elders (ACT 11:30, 15:2-23) reflects the institutional-maturation of the Jerusalem-church by ~AD 45-46. This verse closes the ACT 9:32-12:24 'church expands to include Gentiles' section (per Acts outline uW FRONT-matter) and transitions toward the Paul-centered ACT 13-28 missionary-expansion narrative. No hapax. No textual variant.
